title: Mughlai Chicken Korma

subs.name: 750 g lamb shoulder, cut in pieces — increase braising time to 35 minutes
2 tbsp thick coconut cream for a dairy-lighter version
replace with extra cashews — the paste will be slightly richer
a drop of kewra (pandanus) essence — different floral note, equally Mughlai

steps.text: Grind soaked cashews and almonds with 4 tbsp water to a smooth, thick paste. Heat ghee in a heavy-bottomed pot on medium. Add bay leaves, cardamom, cinnamon, and cloves. Let sizzle for 30 seconds.
Add the sliced onions and fry on medium-low for 15–18 minutes, stirring frequently, until they are soft, golden, and completely collapsed — not brown, just deeply sweet. Do not rush this step.
Add ginger-garlic paste and fry for 2 minutes. Add the nut paste and cook for 4 minutes until it thickens and the ghee separates at the edges.
Reduce heat to low. Add the whisked curd one spoonful at a time, stirring constantly between each addition so it does not curdle. Once all the curd is incorporated, add the chicken and turn to coat.

Season with salt and garam masala. Cover tightly and cook on low flame for 20–22 minutes, stirring twice, until the chicken is completely cooked through and the oil surfaces around the edges of the gravy.
Stir in cream and saffron (if using). Simmer uncovered for 3 minutes to bring the gravy together. Finish with a few drops of rose water. Serve with naan, sheermal, or steamed basmati.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep raw animal products, their plates and utensils separate from cooked food; wash hands and surfaces after contact.
critical_checks: Verify poultry doneness with a food thermometer where possible; do not rely on colour alone.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: almond → replace with extra cashews — the paste will be slightly richer; chicken → 750 g lamb shoulder, cut in pieces — increase braising time to 35 minutes; cream → 2 tbsp thick coconut cream for a dairy-lighter version; rose-water → a drop of kewra (pandanus) essence — different floral note, equally Mughlai
